data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children once more; third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260512T024606-44dcd578/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing child IDs, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a success. Do not mix implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260512T024606-44dcd578/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

`.ddx/executions/20260512T024606-44dcd578/no_changes_rationale.txt` must contain one:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` reject.

To request orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260512T024606-44dcd578/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260512T024606-44dcd578/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and project defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-170bea78">
    <title>docs(operator): document harness-mixing for parallel ddx work shells</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
  The 2026-05-09 plan-review opus self-review identified a gap in the
  operator playbook: when running multiple ddx work --once shells in
  parallel (after ddx-1f774edf lands), operators must understand that
  using the same harness across all N workers will throttle on the
  per-account rate limit (e.g., Anthropic 5-tier rate limits for the
  claude harness). Mixing harnesses across workers (claude + codex +
  openrouter, etc.) dodges per-harness ceilings and delivers true N×
  throughput. There is no operator documentation today that says this.

CURRENT STATE
  - CLAUDE.md describes the project structure and per-CLI commands but
    not the operator playbook for parallel drains.
  - docs/helix/06-iterate/ contains reliability principles and alignment
    reviews but no operator-facing parallel-drain guide.
  - .agents/skills/ddx/reference/ and .claude/skills/ddx/reference/
    contain skill documentation but not an explicit
    parallel-drain-playbook section.
  - CLAUDE.md memory note feedback_models.md says "prefer claude harness
    for execute-loop; avoid agent harness for real work" — the harness
    selection guidance exists for single-worker drain but not for
    parallel.

PROPOSED FIX
  Add a short section to CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/ if
  preferred for separation of concerns) titled "Parallel drain
  operator playbook" covering:

  1. When to use parallel drain: after ddx-1f774edf lands; not before
     (lock contention defeats the parallelism today).

  2. How to start: open N tmux panes / shells, each running a
     `ddx work --once` loop. No code change required.

  3. Harness mixing: example of running claude on one shell, codex on
     another, openrouter on a third — mixing dodges per-harness rate
     limits and avoids serializing on Anthropic quota.

  4. Cost ceiling: each ddx work invocation accepts --max-cost; budget
     across N shells.

  5. Conflict expectations: parallel landing produces some preservation
     under load (covered by the chaos sister bead); set operator
     expectation that occasional preservation is normal and the
     preserved iteration refs can be merged manually.

  6. Observability: point at the lock-metrics sister bead's output
     channels (once landed) for debugging contention.

NON-SCOPE
  - Building a `ddx work --parallel N` flag (deliberately YAGNI per
    plan review; external shells deliver the same throughput).
  - Server-spawned-worker design (ADR-022 step 7, separate work).
  - Harness-selection algorithm (single-worker memory note already
    covers default selection).

INTERSECTIONS
  - Sister beads: ddx-1f774edf (must land before parallel drain is
    safe), the chaos test bead, the lock-metrics bead.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. CLAUDE.md (or docs/helix/06-iterate/parallel-drain-playbook.md) contains a "Parallel drain operator playbook" section covering the 6 items in PROPOSED FIX.
2. The doc explicitly notes that parallel drain is unsafe before ddx-1f774edf lands (because of withMainGitLock spanning network I/O).
3. The doc gives a concrete example of mixing harnesses (claude + codex + openrouter or equivalent) across N shells.
4. The doc cross-references the chaos test bead and the lock-metrics bead.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes (markdown lint + secrets + ddx-config validation are the relevant gates for a doc-only change).
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:6, area:docs, area:operator, kind:doc, prevention</labels>
    <metadata base-rev="ff0fd049898353bee4a1fb75fb87dee3315d3fb5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260512T024606-44dcd578"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

Added a new operator guide at [docs/helix/06-iterate/parallel-drain-playbook.md](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-170bea78-20260512T024606-44dcd578/docs/helix/06-iterate/parallel-drain-playbook.md#L1) covering:

- when parallel drain is safe, and why it is unsafe before `ddx-1f774edf` lands
- how to run `N` external `ddx work --once` shells
- how to mix harnesses across shells, with a concrete `claude` / `codex` / `openrouter` example
- per-shell `--max-cost` budgeting
- expected preservation under load, with a cross-reference to `ddx-42741260`
- lock-contention observability, with a cross-reference to `ddx-128c7f9d`

Verification passed:

- `lefthook run pre-commit`

Committed as:

- `ec026d3c5` `docs: add parallel drain operator playbook [ddx-170bea78]`
